description_long: This is a large, irregularly shaped stone with a roughly vertical orientation and a somewhat rounded top. Its surface is uneven, featuring a mix of smooth and coarse textures, with some visible pitting and minor indentations, especially near the base. The stone appears to have a natural, weathered look, with no visible artificial carvings or markings. One side shows a more jagged and eroded section, possibly from natural chipping or breakage, while the other sides are more continuous and less fragmented. The base is uneven and slightly undercut, suggesting it may have been partially buried or exposed to erosion. This stone would be a prominent obstacle or barrier in an urban park, roadside, or landscaped area.
description: A large, irregular, weathered stone with a rough, pitted surface and a rounded top, featuring jagged and eroded sections near the base.
description_view_0: The stone appears tall and upright, with a mostly smooth face and some roughness along the left edge.
description_view_1: This view shows the broadest, most continuous surface, with gentle curves and minimal jaggedness.
description_view_2: The stone's right side is visible, showing a more vertical profile with subtle surface undulations.
description_view_3: This angle reveals the most eroded and jagged section near the base, with visible holes and undercut areas.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or barrier on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular pitting and minor surface undulations, with no artificial carvings or regular veining.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered, with visible erosion and minor undercutting at the base, indicating prolonged exposure to natural elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with manicured urban surfaces but may blend with natural landscaping or other stones; could be partially surrounded by grass or soil.
